calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Maureen McFadden, via ZOOM, counsel for Timothy Breen, via ZOOM, Administrator; Robert Garcia, in-person, in propria persona.

At the outset, the court queries Mr. Garcia as to the status of his objection. Mr. Garcia advises that he has been unable to find counsel who will represent him and thus has not been able to draft and file his papers and requests that the court consider his objections without formally filing papers. The court advises Mr. Garcia that his objections will need to be formally filed and served on the opposition before the court can consider them. Ms. McFadden replies that she believes that Mr. Garcia has had ample time to draft and file his papers.

After additional discussion, the Court takes the Accounting Petition under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Continued To Apr-26-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for and with Timothy Breen (Administrator) by zoom.
Robert Garcia appeared in Court in Propria Persona.

Mr. Garcia stated that he is still objecting, that he wasn't able to find an Attorney to represent him and that the response he is getting from law firms is that they are not taking any contingency matters. The Court informed Mr. Garcia that he needs to file written objections and serve them on Ms. McFadden, and that if it is not done on time, the objections will be overruled and won't be considered. Ms. McFadden stated that this is the second continuance, that having this heard sooner rather than later would be great, that there is no money in the estate and that payments are not being made on the property. Mr. Garcia stated that this has been very difficult for him, that he is really concerned, that he is continuing to look for an Attorney, but he keeps getting the same response, that the Attorney he had was conuter productive and that he wouldn't know how to do or file a petition. Ms. McFadden stated that Mr. Garcia had an Attorney, but she was asked to be removed, that Mr. Garcia has been uncooperative with the Attorney and the Administrator, that Mr. Garcia has broken in to the apartment and has costed the estate lots of money, and that the HOA has made it very clear that it will continue to fine the estate. Mr. Garcia stated that he needed his medications, that he would not do anything to harm the apartment, that his only objection was to the Brother-in-Law having used his credit card for the apartment, that he has asked about the costs and expressed concern about all parties being in the same group. Ms. McFadden stated that she is happy to send copies of everything to Mr. Garcia. The Court informed Mr. Garcia that he needs to get his objections in writing to Ms. McFadden so that she can react. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to April 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Garcia must file his objections no later than April 12, 2021. If nothing is filed, the Court will go forward on the petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-08-2021 Continued To Mar-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-01-2021 Continued To Mar-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Maureen McFadden appeared by zoom for and with Timothy Breen (Administrator) by zoom.
Robert Garcia (Partner of the Decedent) appeared in Court in Propria Persona.

Mr. Garcia stated that he has been unable to get an Attorney replacement, that he is objecting to the petition, that he spoke with a firm on Friday and that he is planning on having an Attorney represent him. Mr. Garcia also stated that he has been trying to get an Attorney since the last hearing and requested a one-month continuance. Ms. McFadden stated that she has no problem with Mr. Garcia getting an Attorney. The Frist and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to March 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-11-2021 Continued To Feb-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-16-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
Off Calendar Nov-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Margaret O'Dowd, Esq., counsel for Robert Garcia, Objector (appearing by video conference); Maureen McFadden, Esq., specially appearing.

The Court notes the purpose of today's hearing and confirms that neither the Court nor Ms. O'Dowd has received objection to the motion to be relieved as counsel for Mr. Garcia. Ms. McFadden notes that she is appearing in this matter only in that she is similarly situated with respect to Mr. Garcia in a related proceeding.

The Court grants the Motion relieving Ms. O'Dowd as counsel for Robert Garcia in this proceeding, signing the Order for same in open court.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kerry Sykes for Patrick McGee (Petitioner).
Margaret O'Dowd for and with Robert Garcia (Petitioner).
Maureen McFadden for and with Timothy Breen (Proposed Administrator).
Rowena Navia for Leo Bautista (Proposed Administrator).

Ms. O'Dowd stated that they have not spoken, that they have some concern, that they want a professional fiduciary with an independent attorney and raised the issue of conflict with Ms. McFadden's representation of Mr. Breen. The Court informed Ms. O'Dowd that ultimately they can not decide who Mr. Breen hires, stated that there are significant procedural issues and that Mr. McGee and Mr. Garcia have no standing. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss the matter. Upon returning, Ms. O'Dowd stated that they have agreed to Mr. Breen being appointed with Ms. McFadden as his Attorney. Mr. Garcia's Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is denied. The Court granted Mr. McGee's Amended Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration appointing Timothy Breen as the Administrator (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $1,030,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for December 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Continued To May-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kerry Sykes for Patrick McGee (Petitioner).
Margaret O'Dowd for and with Robert Garcia (Beneficiary).

Ms. O'Dowd requested a continuance, stated that she filed her objections yesterday, that she would like to file a competing petition and that her Client wants a Private Fiduciary. Ms. O'Dowd also stated that Mr. Garcia has a right of occupancy, that there is a complicated issue of who will pay the expenses and that the taxes are being paid with the Decedent's credit card. Ms. Sykes stated that the question is who has the power to appoint an administrator, that she believes the siblings have the power, that it's an allowance for the right to inhabit, that Mr. Garcia is not a devisee, that he does not take and that he has no priority. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to May 15, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Continued To Apr-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kerry Sykes for and with Patrick McGee (Petitioner).
Margaret O'Dowd for and with Robert Garcia (Beneficiary).

The Court questions why nomination of the Petitioner was not done by Kathleen and raised the issue of bond. Ms. Sykes stated that Kathleen does not have priority to nominate, that the relatives entitled to nominate are Patrick Concannon and his Siblings, that Patrick and his Siblings are entitled to receive the entirety of the estate and that the Decedent's Mother is receiving nothing. Ms. Sykes also stated that Patrick and all of the Siblings are Ireland citizens and are not eligible to serve, that Mr. McGee has no legal conflict with the Beneficiaries and that he is well respected in the Irish community. In addition, Ms. Sykes expressed concern stating that there is only $30,000.00 in the estate and that the cost of having a bond may be $1,000.00 a year. Ms. O'Dowd stated that her Client objects to the appointment of Mr. McGee, that he has not filed a petition and that based on the relationship he believes Mr. McGee will not treat him fairly. Ms. Sykes stated that Mr. Garcia has no standing. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to April 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
